questions.text: Which metal is liquid at room temperature?
questions.explanation: Mercury (Hg) is the only metal that is liquid at room temperature. It has a low melting point of -38.83°C.
Non-metals form acidic oxides. Metals form basic oxides. For example, CO₂ is acidic, while Na₂O is basic.

questions.correctAnswer: An alloy is a homogeneous mixture of two or more metals or a metal and a non-metal.
questions.explanation: An alloy is made by mixing metals to improve properties. Examples: Brass (copper + zinc), Bronze (copper + tin), Steel (iron + carbon).

questions.text: The ability of metals to be drawn into wires is called ________.
questions.explanation: Ductility is the property of metals that allows them to be drawn into thin wires. Gold and silver are highly ductile.
